DISCOVERY 302, TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF GIORGIANA COOPER'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER. RULING: PLAINTIFF HAS A RIGHT OF PRIVACY UNDER THE CALIF. CONSTITUTION AS TO HER PERSONNEL RECORDS FROM FORMER EMPLOYERS. DEFENDANT ALSO HAS A RIGHT TO DISCOVER EVIDENCE THAT MAY SUPPORT ITS DEFENSES IN THIS CASE. THE COURT BELIEVES THAT THE MOST APPROPRIATE WAY TO RESOLVE THIS MOTION IS TO CONDUCT AN IN-CAMERA REVIEW AS REQUIRED BY EL DORADO SAV. V. SUP. COURT. THE COURT WILL ISSUE AN ORDER SHORTLY WITH THE LOGISTICS FOR THE IN-CAMERA REVIEW. AFTER REVIEWING THE DOCUMENTS, THE COURT WILL ISSUE A TENTATIVE DECISION AND SCHEDULE A HEARING TO ALLOW ARGUMENT IF REQUESTED BY THE PARTIES. PRO TEM JUDGE: DAVID MCDONAL; NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING ON DEFENDANT BLOOMINGDALE'S INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DISCRIMINATION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS SHIFTED THE BURDEN BY PROVIDING EVIDENCE THAT IT HAD LEGITIMATE BUSINESS REASONS FOR ITS ACTIONS WITH RESPECT TO PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PROVIDED SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE TENDING TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANT ACTED WITH A DISCRIMINATORY MOTIVE BASED ON HER RACE. THE MOST SHE CAN SHOW IS THAT SHE (AND PERHAPS ONE OTHER EMPLOYEE) FELT THERE WAS DISCRIMINATION, BUT SHE POINTS TO NO CONCRETE EVIDENCE SUPPORTING THAT BELIEF SUCH AS RACIAL SLURS OR COMMENTS OR ANY OF THE OTHER SORTS OF EVIDENCE ON WHICH OTHER COURTS HAVE RELIED. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS PROVIDED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT WITH RESPECT TO WHETHER DEFENDANT RETALIATED AGAINST PLAINTIFF FOR FILING A CLAIM WITH THE EEOC. ALTHOUGH TEMPORAL PROXIMITY ALONE IS NOT ENOUGH, ACCORDING PLAINTIFF THE BENEFIT OF ALL INFERENCES A REASONABLE JURY MIGHT DRAW, THE CLOSENESS IN TIME OF DEFENDANT'S DISCIPLINARY ACTION TO PLAINTIFF'S EEOC FILING COMBINED WITH DEFENDANT WAITING AN UNCHARACTERISTIC LENGTH OF TIME TO TAKE ACTION AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND DOING SO ONLY AFTER THE EEOC CLAIM IS SUFFICIENT TO SURVIVE THIS MOTION. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL TERMINATION IS DENIED FOR THE SAME REASON. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE ISSUE OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS PROVIDED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT WITH RESPECT TO WHETHER MS. ROCK QUALIFIES AS A "MANAGING AGEN

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-23-2013 Continued To Aug-05-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Bloomingdale'S Inc.'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Courtney Menges, Esq. of Macy's Inc. Law Dept., Tel. 314.342.6454, and Susan T. Kumagai, Esq. of Lafayette & Kumagai, Tel. 415.357.4600, for defendant and moving party;
Daniel Feder, Esq. of the Law Offices of Daniel Feder, Tel. 415.341.94, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: ARGUED. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on Defendant Bloomingdale's Inc.'s Motion For Summary Judgment or, in the alternative, Summary Adjudication. The motion for summary judgment is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is Granted in part and Denied in part. The motion for summary adjudication on the third cause of action for discrimination is granted. Defendant has shifted the burden by providing evidence that it had legitimate business reasons for its actions with respect to Plaintiff. Plaintiff has not provided substantial evidence tending to show that Defendant acted with a discriminatory motive based on her race. The most she can show is that she (and perhaps one other employee) felt there was discrimination, but she points to no concrete evidence supporting that belief such as racial slurs or comments or any of the other sorts of evidence on which other courts have relied. The motion for summary adjudication on the first cause of action for retaliation is denied. Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence to create a triable issue of material fact with respect to whether Defendant retaliated against Plaintiff for filing a claim with the EEOC. Although temporal proximity alone is not enough, according Plaintiff the benefit of all inferences a reasonable jury might draw, the closeness in time of Defendant's disciplinary action to Plaintiff's EEOC filing combined with Defendant waiting an uncharacteristic length of time to take action against Plaintiff and doing so only after the EEOC claim is sufficient to survive this motion. The motion for summary adjudication on the second cause of action for wrongful termination is denied for the same reason. The motion for summary adjudication on the issue of punitive damages is denied. Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence to create a triable issue of material fact with respect to whether Ms. Rock qualifies as a "managing agent." Moving party to prepare an order. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

No appearances. This motion will be heard at 9:00am in Department 301 on Line 3 of the Discovery Calendar. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR #5976

Plaintiff'S Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Jul-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted.
GIORGIANA COOPER'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER.
Ruling: Plaintiff has a right of privacy under the Calif. Constitution as to her personnel records from former employers. Defendant also has a right to discover evidence that may support its defenses in this case. The Court believes that the most appropriate way to resolve this motion is to conduct an in-camera review as required by El Dorado Sav. v. Sup. Court. The Court will issue an order shortly with the logistics for the in-camera review. After reviewing the documents, the Court will issue a tentative decision and schedule a hearing to allow argument if requested by the parties. Pro Tem Judge: David McDonal; Not reported
